name: 2-Light Continuous + Rectangular Softbox (Small Studio) — Dramatic (Deep Skin)
why_this_works: Rect softbox gives controlled falloff and edge definition. Continuous lets you see shadows in real time, which is huge for dramatic shaping on deep skin tones.
failure_modes: Not enough output if you want f/8+ at ISO 100
Mixed ambient can contaminate color
Softbox too close can flatten contrast

substitutions.tradeoff: Harder edge and faster falloff; more dramatic but less forgiving.

name: Monolight Strobe + Beauty Dish (Small Studio) — Dramatic (Deep Skin)
why_this_works: Beauty dish gives crisp highlight transitions and punch. Deep skin holds specular highlights beautifully when the dish is feathered and not blasted straight-on.
failure_modes: Hot spots if dish is aimed directly at face
Too much shine on forehead/cheeks without powder control
Harsh shadows if fill is missing

substitutions.tradeoff: Softer transitions; less punch and less sculpting.

name: Monolight Strobe + Strip Softbox (Small Studio) — Dramatic Rim/Edge (Deep Skin)
why_this_works: Stripbox makes clean edge separation and shape—ideal for dramatic portraits where you want jawline and cheekbone definition on deep skin.
failure_modes: Edge light can blow out hair/shoulders if too close or too high power
Spill onto background in small rooms
Uneven rim if subject turns

substitutions.tradeoff: Less control; rim becomes broader and less defined.

name: 2-Speedlight + Rect Softbox (Small Studio) — Dramatic (Deep Skin)
why_this_works: Affordable dramatic setup: key in rect softbox for shape, second speedlight as low-power fill or background control. Deep skin benefits from controlled specular and clean shadow direction.
failure_modes: Speedlights struggle at low ISO / small apertures
Recycle time limits fast shooting
Inconsistent color if batteries sag

substitutions.tradeoff: Less control; more spill and flatter contrast.

name: Monolight Strobe + Reflective Umbrella (Small Studio) — Dramatic (Deep Skin)
why_this_works: Reflective umbrella is punchier than shoot-through and easier to flag. In small rooms it’s a good dramatic fallback when you need more direction without a softbox.
failure_modes: Still spills light without flags
Hot center if umbrella is cheap
Background can lift unintentionally

substitutions.tradeoff: Softer and flatter; less drama and less control.

name: 2-Speedlight + Reflective Umbrella (Small Studio) — Dramatic (Deep Skin)
why_this_works: Portable dramatic setup. Reflective umbrella keeps directionality while second speedlight can add separation or subtle fill. Great when space is tight and budget matters.
failure_modes: Power ceiling is real—watch ISO/aperture expectations
Umbrella spill can reduce drama without flags
TTL can drift; manual is safer

substitutions.tradeoff: Better control and drama but slower setup.

name: Basic 2-Light + Rect Softbox (Small Studio) — Dramatic (Deep Skin)
why_this_works: This is the clean dramatic baseline: key light sculpting from rect softbox + second light for separation or controlled fill. Deep skin reads rich with shaped shadows and controlled specular.
failure_modes: Too much fill kills drama
Key too frontal flattens face
Background separation missing without a rim or grid

substitutions.tradeoff: More punch and specular; can get harsh fast in small rooms.

name: Basic 2-Light + Beauty Dish (Small Studio) — Dramatic (Deep Skin)
why_this_works: Beauty dish for dramatic, high-contrast shaping + second light for separation. Deep skin handles specular well when the dish is feathered and the fill is minimal.
failure_modes: Specular hotspots if too close
Shadow density can get too heavy without reflector/fill
Small room bounce can reduce contrast

substitutions.tradeoff: Softer and more forgiving; slightly less dramatic.
